concept_pairs.title: The Letter She Never Sent
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Vilhelm Hammershøi — Danish master of muted domestic interiors, figures turning away, light through gauze
concept_pairs.medium: Oil on linen canvas with visible tooth, muted gray-gold palette, soft edges, cracked varnish effect
concept_pairs.critique: Strong emotional core but risks being too quiet for competition. Needs the letter itself to be a visual anchor — perhaps a touch of red in the stamp?
concept_pairs.refined_direction: Lean into the Hammershøi gray but add one warm element — the morning coffee steam catching light

concept_pairs.title: The Door Left Open
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Andrew Wyeth — American loneliness, threshold moments, the poetry of leaving
concept_pairs.medium: Tempera on panel, dry-brush technique, high contrast between interior shadow and exterior light
concept_pairs.critique: The silhouette is powerful but may lose face detail. Consider showing just enough face — the edge of a profile, the tension in the jaw.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: Back-lit figure with rim light defining the edge of face/shoulder, tempera's matte quality enhances the stillness

concept_pairs.title: Mother's Hands, Flour Dusted
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Chardin — French master of domestic still life, the sacred in the ordinary
concept_pairs.medium: Classical oil technique with impasto in flour highlights, warm kitchen palette, chiaroscuro from single window
concept_pairs.critique: Risk of being too sentimental. The paused hands must feel arrested by memory, not posed for sentiment.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: The wedding ring should be the sharpest element — everything else slightly soft, as if seen through tear-blurred eyes

concept_pairs.title: The Last Light in the Hall
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Gregory Crewdson — cinematic staging, suburban uncanny, psychological narratives in light
concept_pairs.medium: Photorealistic digital painting with film grain, cinematic aspect ratio implied, practical light source effect
concept_pairs.critique: The partial figure risks being gimmicky. The shadow and dress edge must feel inevitable, not clever.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: Let the hallway itself be the subject — the doors, the wood floor, the dust — with the human presence as punctuation

concept_pairs.title: The Mirror Knows
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Francesca Woodman — self-portraiture, blur, presence and absence, the female gaze
concept_pairs.medium: Photogravure simulation, soft focus at edges, bathroom tiles rendered with porcelain precision
concept_pairs.critique: The fogged mirror is visually strong but conceptually common. Needs a detail that makes it specific — the single toothbrush, the cracked tile.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: The cleared space in the fog should be imperfect — palm print visible, as if she just did it

concept_pairs.title: His Father's Chair
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Lucian Freud — psychological weight of objects, flesh and furniture equally intimate
concept_pairs.medium: Oil on canvas with visible brushwork in leather texture, muted palette with leather brown as warmest note
concept_pairs.critique: Strong concept but risks literalness. The man in the doorway could be too obvious — consider removing him entirely, leaving only the chair's wear-marks to imply decades of presence.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: The chair as portrait of the absent father. No figure. Just the afternoon light and the impression of a body long gone.

concept_pairs.title: The Photograph in the Drawer
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Gerhard Richter — the blurred photograph, memory as incomplete image
concept_pairs.medium: Photo-painting technique, sharp hands holding soft/blurred photo back, bed soft in background
concept_pairs.critique: The withheld image is powerful but needs the posture to carry the emotion. The slump of shoulders, the stillness of spine.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: Her posture should echo the unseen photo — perhaps she's looking at a picture of herself at this exact age

concept_pairs.title: The Window She Watches From
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Rene Magritte — the uncanny ordinary, surrealism through stillness, impossible light
concept_pairs.medium: Oil on canvas, hyper-realistic rendering of mundane objects, surreal light logic, smooth surface
concept_pairs.critique: The upward rain and out-of-sync reflection may be too overtly surreal for 'familiar uncanny.' Subtlety is key.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: Keep only one surreal element — the reflection turned toward us. Let everything else be naturalistic.

concept_pairs.title: The Meal Set for Two
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Edward Hopper — loneliness that isn't lonely, stillness that speaks, American quiet
concept_pairs.medium: Oil with warm underlayer visible through cool surface, dramatic tonal contrast, brushwork visible in candlelight
concept_pairs.critique: The burned-down candles and dust are excellent but risk melodrama. Her expression must be tender, not tragic.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: She has accepted, not resigned. The light should be golden, not funereal. This is memory as comfort, not wound.

concept_pairs.title: The Suitcase by the Door
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Balthus — suspended moments, the weight of interior spaces, figures caught between
concept_pairs.medium: Tempera on board, muted earth tones, slightly flattened perspective, formal composition
concept_pairs.critique: The sitting-on-stairs is visually compelling but the 'waiting' needs specificity. What is she waiting FOR?
concept_pairs.refined_direction: Perhaps a taxi visible through the door's glass panel — headlights suggesting presence, but she doesn't move toward it

concept_pairs.title: The Garden Through Glass
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Édouard Vuillard — pattern, memory, the domestic as theatre, layered intimacy
concept_pairs.medium: Oil with decorative patterning in interior elements, vivid garden rendered in near-impressionist strokes
concept_pairs.critique: The winter/summer dichotomy is beautiful but may read as too fantastical. Ground it with specificity.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: Her breath fog on the glass should be the only truly visible breath — she is exhaling memory

concept_pairs.title: The Book She Stopped Reading
concept_pairs.artist_influence: Rinko Kawauchi — soft light, intimate scale, the sacred in small things
concept_pairs.medium: Soft-focus photographic quality, pre-dawn blue palette, shallow depth of field simulation, grain
concept_pairs.critique: The dried tears risk sentimentality. Better: the tear-tracks have dried but her face is peaceful — she's processed something.
concept_pairs.refined_direction: The bookmark on the floor is crucial — it speaks of intention abandoned, of a night that changed direction
